global_context.scene_description: A darkly humorous advertising image styled as a newspaper obituary page. At the very top of the image, large bold black text in a heavy condensed font reads 'DON'T WAIT FOR THE FUNERAL.' on a slightly off-white newspaper-textured background. Below the headline, the main visual is a realistic newspaper page laid on a dark wooden table surface, slightly crumpled and coffee-stained. The newspaper is open to the obituaries section. The central obituary is prominently displayed with a small black-and-white photo of an old, rusty HVAC unit where a person's portrait would normally be. The obituary text reads: 'In Loving Memory of Your Old AC Unit, 2011-2026. Died doing what it loved: making weird noises and running up your electric bill. Survived by a homeowner who should have replaced it sooner. In lieu of flowers, please send $1,400 off a new system.' The obituary is circled in red marker. A pair of reading glasses sits on the newspaper. A coffee mug with a ring stain is nearby. The overall lighting is warm and domestic, like a kitchen table in morning light. At the very bottom of the image is a solid bright red horizontal banner spanning full width with bold white uppercase text reading '$1,400 OFF A NEW SYSTEM. BEFORE IT'S TOO LATE.' and the ASH Cooling & Heating logo in white on the right side.

composition.focal_point: The obituary with the HVAC unit photo and the red marker circle around it

content: In Loving Memory of Your Old AC Unit, 2011-2026
Died doing what it loved: making weird noises and running up your electric bill.
Survived by a homeowner who should have replaced it sooner.
In lieu of flowers, please send $1,400 off a new system.
$1,400 OFF A NEW SYSTEM. BEFORE IT'S TOO LATE.

semantic_relationships: Headline at top sets the dark humor tone immediately
Newspaper fills the frame below, creating an immersive obituary reading experience
The HVAC unit photo where a person's portrait would be is the comedic twist
Red marker circle draws the eye to the specific obituary among the page
Reading glasses and coffee mug create a domestic morning scene — this is someone's kitchen table
The obituary copy is the punchline: 'Died doing what it loved: making weird noises'
Red banner at bottom pivots from humor to offer — the solution to avoiding this fate
